metadata.summary: Google Ads API patterns for bidding strategy management, conversion action setup, offline conversion upload, and GA4 integration to enable smart bidding optimization.

content.title: Google Ads Optimization — Bidding, Conversions, GA4
content.summary: Google Ads API patterns for bidding strategy management, conversion action setup, offline conversion upload, and GA4 integration: create portfolio bidding strategies (Target CPA, Target ROAS), apply them to campaigns, set device bid adjustments, define conversion actions with counting and attribution, upload offline conversions via gclid, and query imported GA4 conversion data. The core rule is: let automated bidding accumulate at least 30 conversions per campaign before switching from Maximize Conversions to Target CPA — insufficient conversion data causes erratic bidding.

why.p: Smart bidding (Target CPA, Target ROAS) outperforms manual CPC once enough conversion signal exists, but the algorithm fails without data. Conversion actions must be correctly typed, counted, and attributed before enabling smart bidding — otherwise the algorithm optimizes for the wrong signal. Offline conversion upload via gclid closes the loop between ad clicks and CRM-recorded revenue, enabling true ROAS measurement for high-consideration purchases.

list.item: Configuring bidding strategies for existing Google Ads campaigns via Python client
Creating or updating conversion actions (WEBSITE, APP, UPLOAD types)
Uploading offline conversions from CRM data matched by gclid
Querying per-campaign conversion volume and value split by conversion action
Setting device-level bid adjustments on campaigns
Initial campaign/ad group/keyword creation — use google-ads-campaign-setup methodology instead
Ad copy and responsive search ad authoring — use google-ads-creative methodology
Cross-channel budget allocation — use budget-optimization methodology
GA4 property administration or event schema design — this covers only the Ads API side

rule.statement: Do not enable Target CPA or Target ROAS until a campaign has recorded at least 30 conversions in the past 30 days — use Maximize Conversions until that threshold is reached.
rule.rationale: Smart bidding requires conversion history to model cost predictions; below 30 conversions the algorithm over-fits to noise, causing erratic CPA swings and potential delivery collapse.

list.item: Manual CPC — full control, no automation
Enhanced CPC — manual plus conversion boost adjustment
Maximize Clicks — traffic focus, no conversion signal needed
Maximize Conversions — conversion focus, no CPA target
Target CPA — requires 30+ conversions per 30 days
Target ROAS — requires conversion value tracking and 50+ conversions per 30 days
Maximize Conversion Value — revenue optimization

section.name: create-target-cpa-portfolio-strategy
section.p: Portfolio bidding strategies are created once and assigned to multiple campaigns, centralizing bid adjustments across the account structure.

example.title: Create Target CPA portfolio strategy
example.code: def create_target_cpa_strategy(client, customer_id, name, target_cpa_micros): bidding_strategy_service = client.get_service("BiddingStrategyService") operation = client.get_type("BiddingStrategyOperation") strategy = operation.create strategy.name = name strategy.type_ = client.enums.BiddingStrategyTypeEnum.TARGET_CPA strategy.target_cpa.target_cpa_micros = target_cpa_micros # CPC ceiling = 2x target CPA to prevent runaway bids strategy.target_cpa.cpc_bid_ceiling_micros = target_cpa_micros * 2 response = bidding_strategy_service.mutate_bidding_strategies( customer_id=customer_id, operations=[operation] ) return response.results[0].resource_name
example.note: The cpc_bid_ceiling_micros prevents the bidder from over-spending on individual clicks; set conservatively to 2x target or the campaign will throttle hard.

section.name: device-bid-adjustment
section.p: Device bid adjustments apply multipliers to the base bid for specific device types. Use to increase bids on high-converting devices (e.g., mobile) or reduce spend on low-intent ones (e.g., tablets).

example.title: Set device bid adjustment
example.code: def set_device_bid_adjustment(client, customer_id, campaign_id, device, modifier): """ device: MOBILE, DESKTOP, TABLET modifier: 1.0 = no change, 1.2 = +20%, 0.8 = -20% """ service = client.get_service("CampaignCriterionService") operation = client.get_type("CampaignCriterionOperation") criterion = operation.create criterion.campaign = f"customers/{customer_id}/campaigns/{campaign_id}" criterion.device.type_ = getattr(client.enums.DeviceEnum, device.upper()) criterion.bid_modifier = modifier response = service.mutate_campaign_criteria( customer_id=customer_id, operations=[operation] ) return response.results[0].resource_name
example.note: A modifier of 1.2 increases the bid by 20%; a modifier of 0.8 decreases by 20%. Test conservatively in 5-10% increments.

list.item: WEBSITE — website purchases, signups, page views
APP_INSTALL — mobile app installs
APP_IN_APP_PURCHASE — in-app purchases
CALL_FROM_ADS — calls directly from ads
STORE_VISIT — physical store visits
UPLOAD — offline conversions matched by gclid

rule.statement: Set counting_type to ONE_PER_CLICK for leads and ONE_PER_CONVERSION for purchases — never use ONE_PER_CONVERSION for lead forms, which inflates conversion counts when users submit multiple times.
rule.rationale: Incorrect counting inflates conversion volume metrics, causing the bidding algorithm to perceive higher performance than actual and over-bid on lower-quality leads.

section.name: create-conversion-action
section.p: Conversion actions define what events count toward campaign optimization. Each action has a name, category, counting type, and attribution window.

example.title: Create conversion action via API
example.code: def create_conversion_action(client, customer_id, name, category, value=None): """ category: PURCHASE, SIGNUP, LEAD, PAGE_VIEW value: default conversion value (None = use dynamic value) """ service = client.get_service("ConversionActionService") operation = client.get_type("ConversionActionOperation") action = operation.create action.name = name action.category = getattr( client.enums.ConversionActionCategoryEnum, category.upper() ) action.type_ = client.enums.ConversionActionTypeEnum.WEBPAGE action.status = client.enums.ConversionActionStatusEnum.ENABLED action.counting_type = ( client.enums.ConversionActionCountingTypeEnum.ONE_PER_CLICK ) action.attribution_model_settings.attribution_model = ( client.enums.AttributionModelEnum.GOOGLE_ADS_LAST_CLICK ) action.click_through_lookback_window_days = 30 if value: action.value_settings.default_value = value action.value_settings.always_use_default_value = False response = service.mutate_conversion_actions( customer_id=customer_id, operations=[operation] ) return response.results[0].resource_name
example.note: The click_through_lookback_window_days should match your actual sales cycle; default 30 days is a safe starting point.

section.name: offline-conversion-upload

rule.statement: Upload offline conversions within 90 days of the original click — Google rejects gclid-matched events older than 90 days.
rule.rationale: The 90-day gclid validity window is a hard API constraint; delayed uploads silently drop and the bidding algorithm never receives the conversion signal.

section.p: Offline conversions are matched to ad clicks via the gclid (Google Click ID) stored at the time of the click. The conversion timestamp, gclid, and conversion action are all required; currency code is recommended even in single-currency accounts.

rule.statement: Import GA4 conversions to Google Ads via the UI (Tools → Conversions → Import → Google Analytics 4 properties) rather than the API — the API cannot initiate new GA4 links.
rule.rationale: GA4-to-Google Ads linking requires OAuth consent from the GA4 account; this cannot be programmatically initiated via the Google Ads API alone and requires the UI flow.

list.item: In GA4: mark the events as conversions in Admin → Events
In Google Ads: Tools → Conversions → New → Import → Google Analytics 4 properties
Select which GA4 conversion events to import
Imported conversions appear in reports within 24 hours

example.title: Query GA4-imported conversions by campaign
example.code: def get_analytics_conversions(client, customer_id, date_range): """date_range: tuple of ('YYYY-MM-DD', 'YYYY-MM-DD')""" ga_service = client.get_service("GoogleAdsService") query = f""" SELECT campaign.name, segments.conversion_action, segments.conversion_action_category, metrics.conversions, metrics.conversions_value FROM campaign WHERE segments.date BETWEEN '{date_range[0]}' AND '{date_range[1]}' AND metrics.conversions > 0 """ response = ga_service.search(customer_id=customer_id, query=query) conversions = [] for row in response: conversions.append({ "campaign": row.campaign.name, "action": row.segments.conversion_action, "category": row.segments.conversion_action_category.name, "conversions": row.metrics.conversions, "value": row.metrics.conversions_value, }) return conversions
example.note: Filter results by conversion_action_category to isolate GA4-imported events from native Google Ads conversions. This is critical for accurate ROAS calculations.

template.purpose: Python function: create Target CPA portfolio bidding strategy
Python function: batch upload offline conversions by gclid
